metadata.summary: A stage-indexed tactic catalog with quantified lift bands for improving conversion at each funnel stage (Top / Middle / Bottom + Onboarding), plus stage-specific checklists and ICE scoring methodology.

content.title: Funnel Optimization Tactics — Basics
content.summary: A stage-indexed tactic catalog with quantified lift bands for improving conversion at each funnel stage (Top of Funnel, Middle of Funnel, Bottom of Funnel, and Onboarding), plus four stage-specific checklists (landing page, signup form, checkout, onboarding) for pre-launch audits. Every tactic must be pinned to a measurable stage event and a current baseline before it enters the test backlog. ICE scoring is mandatory; never rank by team preference.

why.p: Funnel drop-off without a tactic catalog produces opinion-driven tests with low win rates. This methodology maps each leak type (headline clarity, form friction, page speed, social proof placement, checkout barriers) to concrete tactics with aggregate lift bands sourced from Formstack, ConversionXL, and Google. Paired with ICE scoring, it turns a funnel analysis into a prioritized sprint backlog within one session.

list.item: Funnel analysis has surfaced a stage leak and you need a quick-win tactic matched to that stage.
Producing a 30-day CRO sprint backlog: form-field reduction, button contrast, social proof placement, copy simplification.
Pre-launch audit: running the four-stage checklist against landing page, signup form, checkout, and onboarding screens.
Generating ICE-scored hypotheses for A/B tests where each hypothesis cites a quantified lift band.
Strategy or PLG model-selection work — route to plg-basics.
Industry-specific or personalization tactics — route to funnel-tactics-advanced.
Funnel mapping, diagnosis, or process work — route to funnel-basics-framework.
Brand or positioning work — this methodology assumes positioning is fixed and only the conversion mechanism is being tuned.

section.name: stage-tactics
section.p: Conversion tactics organized by funnel stage (Top of Funnel, Middle of Funnel, Bottom of Funnel) with aggregate lift bands, plus six high-impact quick wins with concrete benchmarks.

section.name: top-of-funnel
section.p: Goal: get users to engage with your product or content. Levers: headline clarity, page speed, ad targeting precision, social proof, value proposition specificity.

list.item: Clearer headlines (one concrete benefit, not feature list): +10-30% engagement.
Page load under 3 seconds: +5-20% conversion (each second after 2s costs ~7%).
Better ad targeting (narrow audience, specific message): +15-40% qualified traffic.
Social proof above fold (user count, logos, testimonials): +5-15% trust signal.
Value prop clarity (what, for whom, why different): +10-25% bounce reduction.

section.name: middle-of-funnel
section.p: Goal: get users to take action (signup, demo, trial). Levers: form field count, progress indicators, trust badges, CTA design, friction removal.

list.item: Reduce form fields to minimum required: +10-25% completion (B2B SaaS typical; B2C lead gen can reach +100%).
Add progress indicators for multi-step flows: +5-15% completion.
Trust badges (security, certifications, guarantees) near CTA: +8-18% conversion.
Action-oriented CTA in high-contrast color: +5-20% click rate.
Remove friction (no credit card required, guest checkout): +15-40% signup rate.

section.name: bottom-of-funnel
section.p: Goal: complete purchase or activation. Levers: guest checkout, payment method variety, error clarity, urgency (must be honest), risk reversal.

list.item: Guest checkout (no forced account creation): +15-30% checkout completion.
Multiple payment options (card, Apple Pay, PayPal): +10-20% checkout completion.
Clear, specific error messages that tell users exactly how to fix the issue: +5-15% form recovery.
Money-back guarantee visible at decision point: +8-15% conversion.
Exit-intent popups (desktop only — no mouse-leave on mobile): +5-12% recovery.

rule.statement: Run these six quick-win tactics first — they are high-impact, low-effort, and have the strongest benchmark evidence. Always validate on mobile and desktop separately; a win on desktop can be a loss on mobile.
rule.rationale: Quick wins build organizational confidence in CRO programs and fund investment in larger experiments. Skipping directly to complex tests wastes momentum.

list.item: Form fields: remove all non-essential fields; gather additional info after signup via progressive profiling. B2B target: 3 fields or fewer for initial signup.
Progress bar: add step indicator to any multi-step process. +15% completion rate.
Button contrast: if CTA is not in top-3 clicked elements on heatmap, increase contrast. High-contrast green/orange: +50% click rate vs low-contrast gray.
Social proof placement: near CTA (+15% conversion) and above fold (+20% trust). User count + logos + 1 testimonial with photo and name is the minimum viable set.
Page speed: target LCP under 2s. Each second over 2s costs 7% conversion. Quick fixes: WebP images, lazy load, CDN, minify CSS/JS.
Copy simplification: replace jargon with one concrete outcome statement. "Ship projects faster with AI planning" outperforms "enterprise-grade, cloud-native workflow optimization solution".

section.name: checklists-and-rules
section.p: Four stage-specific optimization checklists, mandatory ICE ranking, mobile segmentation rule, statistical significance enforcement, and antipatterns.

section.name: stage-checklists
section.p: Landing page checklist: clear headline (1 benefit, under 10 words); subheadline names target audience; hero image/video shows product in use; social proof above fold; single primary CTA; mobile responsive; loads under 3 seconds.

Signup form checklist: 3 or fewer fields for initial signup; value proposition visible near form; social login options (Google, GitHub); progress indicator if multi-step; clear error messages; "what happens next" explanation.

Checkout checklist: guest checkout available; all costs shown upfront (no surprise fees); multiple payment methods; security badges visible; easy to edit cart; clear refund policy; auto-save progress.

Onboarding checklist: welcome with clear single next step; 3 or fewer onboarding screens; skip option always available; core value demonstrated immediately; contextual tips triggered by actions (not upfront tutorial); progress saved automatically.

rule.statement: Every tactic in the test backlog must be pinned to: (1) a measurable stage event, (2) a current baseline rate, and (3) a target lift band. Reject any tactic without all three.
rule.rationale: "Improve copy" without a conversion metric and baseline is not a testable hypothesis. Unpinned tactics produce inconclusive tests and wasted sprint capacity.

rule.statement: Always test and report tactics on mobile and desktop segments separately. Never report a combined conversion rate when segments represent materially different surfaces.
rule.rationale: A form-field reduction that lifts desktop by 20% can drop mobile by 10% if the mobile layout does not adapt. Combined metrics mask the mobile regression.

rule.statement: Cap concurrent A/B tests on the same page to one. Do not start the next test until the current one has reached statistical significance (95% confidence, minimum sample size met).
rule.rationale: Running two tests on the same page at the same time prevents isolating the effect of either change. This is the "testing too many things" error that inflates false-positive rate.

rule.statement: Urgency and scarcity tactics (countdown timers, low-stock messages) must reflect real constraints. Never generate fake countdown timers or inventory counts.
rule.rationale: Fake urgency is deceptive, creates legal and brand risk, and undermines trust when users discover the timer resets on refresh.

antipattern.description: Optimizing small funnel leaks before fixing the largest drop-off stage.
antipattern.reason: Fixing a 5% checkout drop-off while a 50% bounce rate on the landing page exists wastes all effort — the checkout improvement cannot compound when most users never reach checkout.

antipattern.description: Recommending "remove all form fields" without a recovery plan for the trimmed fields.
antipattern.reason: Progressive profiling — gathering removed fields after signup via in-app prompts — must be specified alongside any field-reduction recommendation or the data is permanently lost.

antipattern.description: Auto-publishing landing page variants without experiment flag and human review.
antipattern.reason: Direct production changes bypass the A/B test framework, contaminate existing tests, and prevent clean rollback if the change causes a regression.

template.purpose: Playwright-based signup form auditor: counts fields, detects social login, checks load time and CTA count against checklist pass criteria.
